summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing for KCSI Meeting Room + Vehicle Booking app. Backend: 25/26 pytest cases pass, covering auth (4 roles + register), RBAC (9 cross-role 403 checks), super-admin user CRUD + password reset, meeting room flow (create/overlap-rejection/approve/check-in time-window/check-out/cancel), full vehicle booking lifecycle (create -> approve -> assign vehicle+driver -> user handover -> admin handover -> user return -> admin return -> completed), unified /bookings/mine + /vehicle-bookings/mine shape, and Mongo _id leak guards on all listing endpoints. Frontend: login -> /hub redirects, /my-bookings unified list renders (desktop table + mobile cards with NO horizontal overflow at 390x844), /car/new loads, /admin is blocked for regular users (redirects to /hub), super-admin can access /admin/users. The single failing backend test is a confirmed SECURITY BUG.

critical.issue: PRIVILEGE ESCALATION: endpoint is decorated with require_admin (which is aliased to require_meeting_admin), so meeting_admin can reset ANY user's password — including super_admin's. Should be require_super_admin like the other /api/users routes (PATCH, POST, DELETE). Verified: meeting_admin@test.com successfully reset a TEST user's password (returned 200).

minor.issue: Does NOT re-check overlap when transitioning back to confirmed (e.g. cancelled -> confirmed) and accepts demotion to 'pending'. Could create double-booking edge case.
No brute-force lockout (playbook expects lockout after 5 fails).
Sets no httpOnly cookie; only Bearer JWT returned. CORS_ORIGINS=* combined with allow_credentials=True is rejected by browsers if cookies are added later.
Past-date validation in /api/bookings uses naive datetime.now() — minor TZ ambiguity (server local). Document expected timezone.

action_items: [CRITICAL] Change POST /api/users/{user_id}/password from `require_admin` (= meeting_admin) to `require_super_admin`. Currently a privilege-escalation vulnerability where meeting_admin can hijack any account.
Optional: re-validate overlap when admin re-confirms a cancelled booking via PATCH /api/bookings/{id}/status; reject 'pending' status updates (only allow confirmed/cancelled/completed transitions).
Optional: implement brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (5 fails) per security playbook.
Optional: tighten CORS_ORIGINS away from '*' if cookie auth is added.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1454 lines — should be split into modules (auth.py, rooms.py, bookings.py, vehicles.py, vehicle_bookings.py, users.py).

Many vehicle/driver endpoints repeat the `if admin['role'] not in CAR_ADMIN_ROLES: 403` check inside the body even though `require_any_admin` was already used as Depends — replace with `Depends(require_car_admin)` (the helper exists at line 1056 but isn't used everywhere). Reduces bug surface and DRY-violations.
PATCH /api/bookings/{id}/status accepts 'pending' which lets an admin demote a confirmed booking back to pending — likely unintentional.
VehicleBookingCreate has no validation that start/end times are well-formed HH:MM, and no past-date check (unlike meeting bookings). Users can create vehicle bookings in the past.
/auth/logout is a no-op stateless endpoint; fine for Bearer JWT but no token revocation/blacklist.
No max length on title/notes/purpose fields — consider Field(max_length=...) to prevent abuse.
_overlap() uses naive string comparison on HH:MM which works only for 24-hour zero-padded times; document that contract or validate format.
VehicleBooking model defaults handover/return_info via Field(default_factory=HandoverInfo) — combined with the manual `setdefault` in _public_booking, this is redundant; pick one path.
test_credentials: All 4 seeded accounts work: admin@roombook.com/Admin@123 (super_admin), meeting_admin@test.com/Meet@123, car_admin@test.com/Car@123, user@roombook.com/User@123. Verified via login.
seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_-prefixed users, rooms, vehicles, drivers, meeting bookings, and vehicle bookings. Most are cleaned up via fixture teardown; some completed bookings remain for visual inspection in /my-bookings (visible in screenshots).
context_for_next_testing_agent: 26 pytest cases live in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (auth, RBAC, user-mgmt, meeting flow, vehicle flow, _id leak). One test (test_meeting_admin_can_reset_password_SECURITY_BUG) currently fails by design — once main agent changes /api/users/{id}/password from require_admin to require_super_admin, the test will pass. Frontend playwright covered: login (user+super), /hub redirect, /my-bookings desktop+mobile (no horizontal overflow at 390px), /car/new loads, /admin blocked for user, /admin/users accessible to super_admin. data-testid coverage on login form is good; add testids on hub tiles and my-bookings tabs/buttons for richer regression.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: At /app/backend/server.py line 112 the alias `require_admin = require_meeting_admin` was added for backward-compat. But the new password-reset endpoint at line 601 uses `Depends(require_admin)`. All other /api/users routes (POST, PATCH, DELETE) correctly use `Depends(require_super_admin)`, so this one route is the odd one out. REPRODUCTION: 1) Login as meeting_admin@test.com/Meet@123, 2) POST /api/users (as super) to create a TEST user, 3) As meeting_admin POST /api/users/{TEST_id}/password {password: 'Hacked123'} — returns 200, 4) Login as TEST user with Hacked123 succeeds. MITIGATION: change line 603 from `admin: dict = Depends(require_admin)` to `admin: dict = Depends(require_super_admin)`.
